news.title: Homebuyers can seek relief for delay after possession: Supreme Court
news.para: Homebuyers can still seek compensation for delayed flat possession even after taking custody, the Supreme Court ruled. The apex court set aside a previous order stating homebuyers cease to be consumers post-possession. It also clarified that arbitration clauses in agreements do not prevent buyers from approaching consumer forums for grievances. This landmark decision revives a 22-year-old case, emphasizing that contractual clauses cannot override statutory remedies.

news.title: Mohali: Suntech City's developer in ED custody in ₹172 crore money laundering case
news.para: Realtor Ajay Sehgal has been remanded to three-day Enforcement Directorate custody in a money laundering case linked to the Suntech City project. The ED alleges Sehgal and others developed the project on land they didn't own, using forged documents for permissions, generating over Rs 172 crore in illicit gains. New facts have emerged, necessitating further questioning to trace the money trail and identify other involved individuals.

news.title: Consumer panel orders Ansal Hi-Tech Townships to refund buyer left without flat after 12 years
news.para: A Noida consumer commission has directed Ansal Hi-Tech Townships Limited to refund a homebuyer Rs 1,00,762 plus 6% interest for failing to deliver possession of a flat booked in 2014. The buyer paid instalments over several years but never received the property. The developer did not respond to the complaint, leading to an ex parte order. The commission also awarded Rs 2,000 for litigation costs and mental distress.

news.title: Authorities issue fresh notices to over a dozen commercial establishments after deadly Lucknow fire
news.para: Following a tragic fire that claimed 15 lives, the Lucknow Development Authority intensified its crackdown on commercial buildings violating approved plans. Over a dozen establishments, including gyms, coaching centers, and restaurants, received show-cause notices. This action aims to ensure compliance with building regulations after the devastating Aliganj blaze, with further inspections planned.

news.title: Chhattisgarh housing board earns ₹1,532 crore revenue from sale of 7,388 properties: CM
news.para: Chhattisgarh's Housing Board has achieved a significant turnaround, generating Rs 1,532 crore from property sales over two years. Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai announced the board's expanded role in infrastructure development, aiming to fulfill housing aspirations. The state has also made substantial progress in the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana, completing over 10.50 lakh houses for the needy.

news.title: NGT asks department to take action against 'illegal' groundwater extraction in Noida, Greater Noida
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has directed the Uttar Pradesh Ground Water Department to investigate allegations of illegal groundwater extraction by builders, industries, and individuals in Noida and Greater Noida.  Environmental activist Vikrant Tongad filed a petition after previous complaints to authorities yielded no action. The tribunal mandates ground verification and appropriate remedial and punitive measures if illegal borewells are found, with the process to be completed within three months.

news.title: Fidelity Investments buys 2% stake in Lodha Developers for ₹1,864 crore
news.para: Fidelity Investments has acquired a 2% stake in Lodha Developers for Rs 1,864 crore through open market transactions. Eight Fidelity affiliates purchased nearly 2 crore shares from two promoter entities, Hightown Constructions and Homecraft Developers. This transaction reduces the promoters' combined holding to 70.28%. Lodha Developers recently reported a 9% rise in consolidated net profit for the March quarter.

news.title: Panel to meet for auditing sanctioned plans for Kolkata high-rises, commercial buildings
news.para: Following a tragic warehouse collapse, West Bengal's Chief Minister has ordered a special audit of all sanctioned high-rise building plans in and around Kolkata. A committee, including experts from RITES and IIT Kharagpur, will scrutinize projects for safety compliance. Construction will resume only after clearance, with a focus on public safety over halting development. The audit also extends to fire safety systems and waterbodies.

news.title: PE investment in real estate drops 23% to $1.13 billion in Jan-Jun: Knight Frank
news.para: Private equity investments in India's real estate sector saw a 23% dip to USD 1.13 billion in the first half of 2026, largely due to global economic shifts. Office properties attracted the lion's share of these investments, while the residential segment experienced a notable decline. Experts attribute this trend to rising global borrowing costs, prompting investors to prioritize execution certainty and realized returns.

news.title: Hero MotoCorp, WeWork lease 3.45 lakh sq ft office space at Worldmark 6 in Delhi
news.para: Hero MotoCorp has leased about 2.31 lakh sq ft, while WeWork India has leased about 1.14 lakh sq ft at Worldmark 6, Aerocity, Delhi. The combined estimated rental payout over the lease tenure is about ₹922 crore, according to documents accessed by Propstack.

news.title: Hybon expects revenue to grow 20-25% in FY27; to invest ₹100 crore in Pilkhuwa facility
news.para: Hybon had recorded revenue of about ₹150 crore in FY26. The company is operating its current manufacturing facility at full capacity and is expanding production through a larger unit in Pilkhuwa, near Hapur.

news.title: Nagpur civic body revives Kachipura eviction drive, 56 commercial units to get 15-day notice
news.para: Nagpur Municipal Corporation is set to resume action against 56 illegal commercial establishments on land leased to Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Vidyapeeth. Following a state government order vacating a decade-old stay, notices will be issued, giving occupants 15 days to remove unauthorized structures. The move targets businesses like restaurants and banquet halls, with no current plans to demolish the adjoining Kachipura slum.

news.title: Smartworks to acquire Singapore-based Workstudio Spaces
news.para: Workstudio Spaces has an operational footprint of about 26,000 sq ft in Singapore. After the proposed acquisition, Smartworks’ Singapore portfolio is expected to expand to four centres with a total footprint of about 76,000 sq ft.

news.title: Work stops at 700 sites across Kolkata; developers, contractors worried as daily loss pegged at ₹25 crore
news.para: Construction halted at nearly 700 Kolkata sites following a stop-work notice after a warehouse collapse. The real estate industry faces daily losses of Rs 20-25 crore, fearing crippling delays and skilled labour migration. Developers are cooperating with authorities, urging a swift review to allow projects by reputable firms to resume, while licensed surveyors warn of structural risks to incomplete work.

news.title: Patna civic body's tax collections rise after first ARV revision
news.para: Patna Municipal Corporation has boosted property tax revenue by increasing the Annual Rental Value by 15%, the first revision in 30 years. This move, effective June 24, 2026, aims to fund infrastructure improvements.  A 5% rebate on lump-sum payments by June 30 for FY 2026-27 is also driving higher collections, with daily receipts surging significantly.

news.title: Launched on May 14, Jharkhand's building regularisation portal gets 4,000 applications
news.para: Jharkhand's ambitious building regularisation drive is off to a slow start, with only 4,000 applications received since its May 14 launch. Confusion over rules, particularly the 25-foot road width requirement, is deterring homeowners. Many are urging the government to extend the July 13 deadline and clarify regulations, while also demanding a change to the plot size limit. Officials promise further examination and clarification.

news.title: Ranchi civic body sets seven-day deadline for fire NOC, Bokaro orders safety audit
news.para: Following a deadly fire in Lucknow, Ranchi and Bokaro authorities are cracking down on fire safety. Property owners in Ranchi have seven days to get Fire No Objection Certificates or face sealing. Bokaro is conducting a comprehensive audit of coaching centres and other establishments, highlighting serious gaps like inadequate exits and a lack of NOCs, with strict action promised for non-compliance.

news.title: Dharwad: Consumer panel raps developers, orders relief to senior citizen in plot registration row
news.para: A senior citizen in Dharwad has won a significant victory against developers who refused to register her residential plot despite receiving substantial payment. The District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission ordered the developers to allot an alternative plot and execute the sale deed within a month. They must also pay Rs 50,000 in compensation and Rs 10,000 for litigation costs, ensuring justice for the aggrieved buyer.

news.title: Dubai’s housing market cools after long red-hot five-year rally
news.para: Dubai’s residential property market is cooling after a five-year rally as buyers become more price-sensitive, negotiate harder and shift toward smaller, lower-cost homes amid higher supply and geopolitical uncertainty. While prices remain higher year-on-year, they have softened quarterly, with annual growth slowing sharply from 17–18% in 2024 to around 4–5%.

news.title: Maharashtra orders probe into stamp duty scam estimated to be worth up to ₹20,000 crore
news.para: Maharashtra's Revenue Minister has ordered a high-level probe into a potential Rs 10,000-20,000 crore stamp duty scam. The investigation will scrutinize adjudication proceedings statewide from April 2021 to March 2026, following reports of alleged misuse of powers to reduce assessed stamp duty. A recent Navi Mumbai case involving a clerk processing over 800 documents in ten days, causing a Rs 13.9 crore loss, has triggered this extensive inquiry.
